quiz.title: Unit 7 Quiz 7, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Empirical and Molecular Formulas
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Empirical Formula Calculations, Molecular Formula Calculations, Percent Composition, Molar Mass Determination

questions.question: A compound is 92.3% carbon and 7.7% hydrogen. What is its empirical formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: C = 92.3 g ÷ 12.01 g/mol = 7.69 mol; H = 7.7 g ÷ 1.008 g/mol = 7.64 mol. Divide by the smaller value: C = 7.69 ÷ 7.64 = 1.01 ≈ 1; H = 7.64 ÷ 7.64 = 1. The empirical formula is $\text{CH}$.

questions.question: A compound contains 69.6% manganese and 30.4% oxygen. What is its empirical formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: Mn = 69.6 g ÷ 54.94 g/mol = 1.267 mol; O = 30.4 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 1.90 mol. Divide by the smaller value: Mn = 1.267 ÷ 1.267 = 1; O = 1.90 ÷ 1.267 = 1.5. Multiply by 2 to obtain whole numbers: Mn = 2, O = 3. The empirical formula is $\text{Mn}_2\text{O}_3$.

questions.question: What is the empirical formula of a compound with 41.4% carbon, 3.5% hydrogen, and 55.1% oxygen?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: C = 41.4 g ÷ 12.01 g/mol = 3.45 mol; H = 3.5 g ÷ 1.008 g/mol = 3.47 mol; O = 55.1 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 3.44 mol. Divide by the smallest value (3.44): C = 3.45 ÷ 3.44 = 1.00; H = 3.47 ÷ 3.44 = 1.01 ≈ 1; O = 3.44 ÷ 3.44 = 1.00. The empirical formula is $\text{CHO}$.

questions.question: What is the empirical formula of a compound that consists of 32.1% aluminum and 67.9% fluorine by mass?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: Al = 32.1 g ÷ 26.98 g/mol = 1.19 mol; F = 67.9 g ÷ 19.00 g/mol = 3.57 mol. Divide by the smaller value: Al = 1.19 ÷ 1.19 = 1; F = 3.57 ÷ 1.19 = 3.00. The empirical formula is $\text{AlF}_3$.

questions.question: A compound has a molar mass of 78 g/mol and empirical formula CH. What is its molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Empirical mass of $\text{CH}$ = 12.01 + 1.008 = 13.02 g/mol. n = molar mass ÷ empirical mass = 78 g/mol ÷ 13.02 g/mol = 6. Molecular formula = $(\text{CH})_6 = \text{C}_6\text{H}_6$.

questions.question: What is the molecular formula of a compound with empirical formula CH₂O and molar mass 180 g/mol?
questions.explanation: Empirical mass of $\text{CH}_2\text{O}$ = 12.01 + 2.016 + 16.00 = 30.03 g/mol. n = 180 g/mol ÷ 30.03 g/mol = 6. Molecular formula = $(\text{CH}_2\text{O})_6 = \text{C}_6\text{H}_{12}\text{O}_6$.

questions.question: A compound has empirical formula NH₂ and molar mass 32 g/mol. What is its molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Empirical mass of $\text{NH}_2$ = 14.01 + 2.016 = 16.03 g/mol. n = 32 g/mol ÷ 16.03 g/mol = 2. Molecular formula = $(\text{NH}_2)_2 = \text{N}_2\text{H}_4$.

questions.question: What is the molecular formula of a compound with 38.7% carbon, 9.7% hydrogen, and 51.6% oxygen, and molar mass 62 g/mol?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: C = 38.7 g ÷ 12.01 g/mol = 3.22 mol; H = 9.7 g ÷ 1.008 g/mol = 9.62 mol; O = 51.6 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 3.225 mol. Divide by smallest value (3.22): C = 3.22 ÷ 3.22 = 1; H = 9.62 ÷ 3.22 = 2.99 ≈ 3; O = 3.225 ÷ 3.22 = 1.00. Empirical formula = $\text{CH}_3\text{O}$ (mass = 31 g/mol). n = 62 g/mol ÷ 31 g/mol = 2. Molecular formula = $\text{C}_2\text{H}_6\text{O}_2$.

questions.question: A compound contains 85.7% carbon and 14.3% hydrogen with molar mass 84 g/mol. What is its molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: C = 85.7 g ÷ 12.01 g/mol = 7.14 mol; H = 14.3 g ÷ 1.008 g/mol = 14.19 mol. Divide by smallest: C = 7.14 ÷ 7.14 = 1; H = 14.19 ÷ 7.14 = 1.99 ≈ 2. Empirical formula = $\text{CH}_2$ (mass = 14.03 g/mol). n = 84 g/mol ÷ 14.03 g/mol = 6. Molecular formula = $\text{C}_6\text{H}_{12}$.

questions.question: A compound is analyzed and found to contain 5.93% hydrogen and 94.07% oxygen by mass. What is its empirical formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: H = 5.93 g ÷ 1.008 g/mol = 5.88 mol; O = 94.07 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 5.88 mol. The mole ratio is 1:1. The empirical formula is $\text{HO}$.

questions.question: A compound is 27.4% sodium, 1.2% hydrogen, 14.3% carbon, and 57.1% oxygen. What is its empirical formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: Na = 27.4 g ÷ 22.99 g/mol = 1.19 mol; H = 1.2 g ÷ 1.008 g/mol = 1.19 mol; C = 14.3 g ÷ 12.01 g/mol = 1.19 mol; O = 57.1 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 3.57 mol. Divide by the smallest value (1.19): Na = 1, H = 1, C = 1, O = 3.57 ÷ 1.19 = 3. The empirical formula is $\text{NaHCO}_3$.

questions.question: A dense, oily liquid is analyzed and found to consist of 2.06% hydrogen, 32.69% sulfur, and 65.25% oxygen by mass. The molar mass of the compound is approximately 98.1 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: H = 2.06 g ÷ 1.008 g/mol = 2.04 mol; S = 32.69 g ÷ 32.06 g/mol = 1.02 mol; O = 65.25 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 4.08 mol. Divide by smallest (1.02): H = 2.04 ÷ 1.02 = 2; S = 1; O = 4.08 ÷ 1.02 = 4. Empirical formula = $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$. Empirical mass = 98.08 g/mol, matching the given molar mass. The molecular formula is $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$.

questions.question: A colorless solid is found to contain 25.93% nitrogen and 74.07% oxygen by mass. Laboratory analysis determines the molar mass of the compound to be approximately 108 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: N = 25.93 g ÷ 14.01 g/mol = 1.85 mol; O = 74.07 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 4.63 mol. Divide by smallest (1.85): N = 1; O = 4.63 ÷ 1.85 = 2.5. Multiply by 2: N = 2, O = 5. Empirical formula = $\text{N}_2\text{O}_5$. Empirical mass = (2 × 14.01) + (5 × 16.00) = 28.02 + 80.00 = 108.02 g/mol, matching the given molar mass. The molecular formula is $\text{N}_2\text{O}_5$.

questions.question: A sample of a gaseous pollutant is found to contain 40.05% sulfur and 59.95% oxygen by mass. The molar mass of the compound is approximately 80.06 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: S = 40.05 g ÷ 32.06 g/mol = 1.25 mol; O = 59.95 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 3.75 mol. Divide by smallest (1.25): S = 1; O = 3.75 ÷ 1.25 = 3. Empirical formula = $\text{SO}_3$. Empirical mass = 32.06 + 48.00 = 80.06 g/mol, matching the given molar mass. The molecular formula is $\text{SO}_3$.

questions.question: A sample of reddish-brown rust is analyzed and found to contain 69.94% iron and 30.06% oxygen by mass. The molar mass of the compound is approximately 159.7 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: Fe = 69.94 g ÷ 55.85 g/mol = 1.252 mol; O = 30.06 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 1.879 mol. Divide by smallest (1.252): Fe = 1; O = 1.879 ÷ 1.252 = 1.5. Multiply by 2: Fe = 2, O = 3. Empirical formula = $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$. Empirical mass = (2 × 55.85) + (3 × 16.00) = 111.7 + 48.0 = 159.7 g/mol, matching the given molar mass. The molecular formula is $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered empirical and molecular formula calculations! 🧪📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the steps for finding mole ratios and determining multipliers.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on converting percentages to moles correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Please review percent composition, mole calculations, and how to determine empirical and molecular formulas.
